perturbation.original_text: All notices required under this Agreement or the Ordinance shall be in writing and shall be deemed validly given, unless otherwise required, when sent by certified mail, return receipt requested, or by a nationally recognized overnight courier, addressed as follows (or any other address the party to be notified may have designated to the sender by like notice):
perturbation.changed_text: All notices required under this Agreement or the Ordinance may be given either in writing and shall be deemed validly given when sent by certified mail, return receipt requested, or by a nationally recognized overnight courier, addressed as follows (or any other address the party to be notified may have designated to the sender by like notice):
perturbation.explanation: This modification changes 'shall be' to 'may be', turning a mandatory written notice requirement into an optional one. It contradicts the intention of ensuring that all critical communications are formally documented and trackable, potentially undermining the agreement's enforceability. This creates an in-text contradiction because elsewhere in the contract, certain actions or deadlines may be triggered by 'notice', and this change introduces uncertainty as to whether that 'notice' is even required in writing.
perturbation.contradicted_text: NOTICE. All notices required under this Agreement or the Ordinance may be given either in writing and shall be deemed validly given when sent by certified mail, return receipt requested, or by a nationally recognized overnight courier, addressed as follows (or any other address the party to be notified may have designated to the sender by like notice): Grantor: Grantee: Town of Vinton Roanoke Gas Company Attention: Town Manager Attention: President 311 S. Pollard Street 519 Kimball Avenue, N.E. Vinton, Virginia 24179 P.O. Box 13007 Roanoke, Virginia 24030 The parties may, by notice given under this Section, designate such other addresses as they may deem appropriate for the receipt of notices under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that all notices 'shall be' in writing, making it mandatory. The changed text modifies this to 'may be,' making written notice optional. The contradicted text repeats the changed text, stating that notices 'may be' in writing, which directly contradicts the initial intent of the agreement to ensure formal documentation. This creates a contradiction because the change introduces uncertainty and undermines the enforceability, making it unclear whether written notice is always required, as the original text implied. This ambiguity can affect the triggering of deadlines or actions based on 'notice', leading to disputes. As per the perturbation explanation, this falls under 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction'.
